extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won Stage 6 from 8th position with 200m remaining, using a late outside slingshot off Kristoff after his team got swamped earlier in the finale.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step positioned early but got swamped by Dimension Data's aggressive approach through two late roundabouts (1km and 400m out).
Kittel's extreme power reserves allow deliberate late positioning (8th at 200m) as viable tactic versus traditional leadout dependency.
Roundabouts at 1km and 400m counterintuitively stretched peloton and reduced crash risk despite appearing more dangerous.
Sabatini's controlled tempo string-out served Kittel better than traditional high-speed leadout in this messy positioning battle.

rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: Second win suggests green jersey viability despite pre-race favoritism for Sagan; raw power compensates for positioning chaos
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: neutral: Consistent second-place finishes taking points but losing 13 points/stage differential makes green jersey defense mathematically difficult
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: neutral: Unwitting launch pad for Kittel's slingshot but Holler's protest suggests team uncomfortable with barriers positioning tactics
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: Commentary identifies him as breakaway threat in medium mountain stages to accumulate green jersey points when pure sprinters dropped
